description: Module supports operation as Root Complex or End Point selected by strap or DEVSTAT override. Mode impacts configuration access, I/O capability, and enumeration role.
dependencies: Strap pins; Device status register access; Reset sequencing

description: Single link supports x1 or x2 operation at 2.5 or 5.0 GT/s per lane. Width cannot be split into two independent x1 links.
dependencies: SerDes refclk quality; Lane routing; Partner capabilities

description: Up to four inbound regions translate PCIe BAR hits to internal bus addresses. Configure before enabling LTSSM to accept host accesses safely.
dependencies: BAR sizing on EP; Internal memory map; Endianness settings

description: Outbound windows map internal bus addresses to PCIe address space. Use for MMIO/DMA style host memory access from device.
dependencies: Remote memory apertures; No-snoop/relaxed ordering policy

description: Configurable byte swap supports big-endian and little-endian CPU integration. Program early in initialization to match system endianness.
dependencies: CPU endianness; Software ABI; IB/OB window programming

description: Link training and status state machine can be enabled and observed. Software enables LTSSM and waits for L0 state to confirm link up.
dependencies: Stable refclk; Proper SerDes settings; Partner presence

description: Module supports MSI reception in RC mode and MSI generation in EP mode. Multiple vectors are grouped with enable, status, and clear registers.
dependencies: SoC interrupt controller routing; Config space MSI capability

description: Legacy INTA/B/C/D signaling is supported for backward compatibility. Prefer MSI when supported by the host.
dependencies: SoC interrupt mapping; Host legacy INT support

description: ASPM states L0s and L1 are supported. Entry and exit latencies depend on link partner and SerDes tuning.
dependencies: Partner ASPM capability; Clock gating policies

description: Module can transmit PM_TURNOFF and PME messages and report related events. Status and raw status registers expose power and reset event bits.
dependencies: System power controller policies; Host OS power management

description: Root Complex can issue downstream hot reset to reinitialize link partners. Command register exposes reset trigger with flush indication.
dependencies: Proper quiesce of traffic; Partner reset tolerance

description: End-to-end CRC can be generated for outbound TLPs and checked for inbound.
dependencies: Host ECRC policy; Firmware configuration sequencing

description: Outbound max payload is 128 B and inbound 256 B with remote read request size up to 256 B. Settings interact with link partner capabilities.
dependencies: Partner advertised capabilities; Memory target alignment

description: Software can access remote Type 0 or Type 1 configuration spaces through a programmable window.
dependencies: Bus topology knowledge; Non-cacheable MMIO mapping

description: Module exposes a standards-compliant local configuration space. Initialize device IDs, class codes, and BARs as required for role.
dependencies: Proper strap-based role; System ID assignments

description: In RC mode a 4 KB I/O window enables legacy I/O TLPs. Prefer MMIO when possible for performance and simplicity.
dependencies: IOBASE register; RC mode

description: Transaction layer automatically manages flow control credits. Monitoring via debug registers can indicate bottlenecks.
dependencies: Credit advertisement; TLPCFG settings

description: The design supports only one Virtual Channel and Traffic Class. Software should not attempt multi-VC negotiation.
dependencies: UNKNOWN

description: PHY supports automatic lane reversal and polarity inversion for routing flexibility.
dependencies: Board routing; SerDes configuration

description: Registers expose detailed link state and PHY indicators. LTSSM state transitions help isolate training failures.
dependencies: Access to chip-level cfg space; Clock present

description: A defined sequence enables and locks the SerDes PLL. Mis-sequencing leads to intermittent link failures.
dependencies: Stable differential refclk; Kicker unlock; Power domain on

description: The PCIe power domain must be enabled before register access. Sequence prior to SerDes and LTSSM configuration.
dependencies: Platform PSC driver or baremetal PSC sequence

description: Chip-level configuration writes require unlocking via a kicker mechanism. Relock after completing critical setup.
dependencies: Privilege level; Secure boot policy

description: The design does not support link state L2. Use L0s/L1 only for ASPM.
dependencies: OS power policy; Firmware configuration

feature_name: No FLR and limited vendor messaging
description: Function Level Reset is not supported in this implementation. Vendor-specific message TLPs are not provided.
dependencies: Error management framework; RC reset control

description: Local and remote configuration and RC I/O windows require 32-bit single, non-burst accesses.
dependencies: MMU/MPU configuration; Driver memory attributes

description: Global TLP attributes can be configured for relaxed ordering and no-snoop.
dependencies: Host coherency domain; Cache policies

description: End-of-interrupt requires writing the specific event number to the EOI register.
dependencies: SoC interrupt controller; ISR/EOI framework

description: Standard PCIe message TLPs are supported for functions like PM and MSI.
dependencies: Correct BDF addressing; Capability enables
